Tel(619) 533-4400  Fax 544-6806
T H E  C I T Y  O F  S A N  D I E G O 
SPECIAL EVENTS GUIDELINES 
I. Purpose
These guidelines are intended to familiarize event promoters, sponsors, organizers
and vendors with information about special events procedures and general fire
safety regulations that are enforced by the Fire-Rescue Department, Fire
Prevention Bureau. The Department is committed to promoting public safety and
assisting 
all parties involved by providing a level of service that will ensure a safe
and successful event.
II. Events requiring a Fire-Rescue Department permit
a) Assembly of 50 or more people within a building not classified under the
building code as a place of assembly
b) Outdoor assembly of 50 or more people within a fenced area
c) Amusement buildings
d) Ballpark events with field occupancy
e) Carnivals and fairs
f) Casino nights
g) Exhibits and trade shows
h) Fire performers
i) Fireworks, pyrotechnics or special effects
j) Gr
ad nights
k) Haunted houses and Ghost walks
l) Model rocket launch
m) Open flame effects before a proximate audience
n) Sports Arena events with floor occupancy
o) Stadium events with field occupancy
p) Temporary membrane structures and tents having an area in excess of 400
square feet
q) Vehicle displays 
within a building or tent
III. Temporary increases in occupant load
Please refer to FPB Policy O-11-4 regarding temporary increases in occupant
load. FPB policies can be obtained at:
http://www.sandiego.gov/fire/services/policies/index.shtml

Community Risk Reduction Division 
Fire-Rescue Department  600 B Street, Suite 1300
 San Diego, CA 92101-4903 Tel (619) 533-4400  Fax 544-6806
IV. Permit application
A special event permit application can be obtained at:
http://www.sandiego.gov/fire/pdf/singleapp.pdf  The completed permit
application, along with dimensioned floor and/or site plans and any other
supporting documentation, can be emailed to sdfdevents@sandiego.gov
The completed permit package shall be submitted at least two weeks prior to the
event. After the application is reviewed, the applicant will be contacted if any
additional information required or corrections to be made. Once the permit
application is signed off, a copy will be sent to the applicant. An on-site
inspection 
by the Department may be required to verify that the conditions are in
accordance with the permit. A fee will be charged at the rate specified in the
current fee schedule.
V. Plans
The minimum page size for plans is 8.5" x 11". We request that plans be drawn
large enough and clear enough for review purposes. Plans shall be submitted with
the Application for Single Event Permit.
A detailed site plan is required for all tent, canopy, and membrane structure
permits, fireworks permits, outdoor special effects, beer gardens, and outdoor
place 
of assembly permits. The site plan must show the entire event site, existing
structures and all exits and entrances. This plan must be legible and drawn to
scale or dimensioned.
A detailed floor plan is required for all tent and membrane structure permits,
exhibits and trade shows, indoor special effects permits and indoor place of
assembly permits. 
The floor plan must show all exits and entrances. This plan
must be legible and drawn to scale or dimensioned.
For more information about required plans and/or the Application for Single
Event Permit, contact the Fire Prevention 
Bureau, Special Events Section by
phone at (619) 533-4400 or by email at sdfdevents@sandiego.gov.
VI. Fire Safety Officer
When in the opinion of the Fire Code Official, one or more Fire Safety Officer(s)
(FSO) may be required during an event. Circumstances requiring FSO(s) may
include, but are not limited to: 
Places of assembly or any other place where
people congregate and exhibitions, displays, contest or activities where there is an
unique public safety concern. A fee will be charged for standby FSO’s at the rate
specified in the current fee schedule.

General Information and Links: Federal, State, Local Governing Agencies  
• Federal Forms 
o Federal Drug Administration (FDA) /Center for Devices of Radiological Health (CDRH)  
▪ Variance Application – A “license” to perform a laser light show(s) in the USA to be 
submitted to the Food and Drug Administration Division of Docket Management: 
• https://www.fda.gov/downloads/AboutFDA/ReportsManualsForms/Forms/UC  
M080788.pdf  
• See attached information: How to Submit Electronically to the FDA’s Division 
of Dockets Management (DDM): 
https://www.regulations.gov/searchResults?rpp=25&po=0&s=FDA-2013-S-
0610&fp=true&ns=true  
▪ Laser Light Show Product Reports   
The “product report” (for both laser shows and projectors) that must be submitted to 
FDA’s Center for Devices of Radiological Health (CDRH)certifying your lasers shows 
and/or projectors are compliant: 
• https://www.fda.gov/downloads/AboutFDA/ReportsManualsForms/Forms/UC  
M081634.pdf  
• Can now be submitted via email to this email address: 
RadHealthCustomerService@fda.hhs.gov 
  
▪ Annual Report – Required report of all laser activity (shows and/or projectors used) from 
July 1 to June 30th each year. By filing this report, a basic variance is auto renewed. 
• https://www.fda.gov/downloads/AboutFDA/ReportsManualsForms/Forms/UC  
M081603.pdf  
▪ Accidental Occurrence Report – Form used to report an accident or incident to FDA 
• https://www.fda.gov/downloads/AboutFDA/ReportsManualsForms/Forms/UC  
M236066.pdf  
• Can now be submitted via email to this email address: 
RadHealthCustomerService@fda.hhs.gov 
 
o Federal Aviation Administration 
▪ FAA Form 7140 Notice of Proposed Outdoor Operations – This form must be completed 
when performing outdoor unterminated laser shows. 
• https://www.faa.gov/documentLibrary/media/form/faa7140-1.pdf  
▪ Submit completed form to one of these following FAA Service Areas:  
• Eastern: 9-ATO-ESA-OSG-Lasers-Searchlights@faa.gov  
• Central: 9-ASW-Operations-Support-Lasers@faa.gov  
• Western: 9-ATO-WSA-OSG-Lasers@faa.gov  
• Record Keeping Forms: 
o Standard Operating Procedures – Description of how laser shows are performed safely 
o Quality Control Safety Checklist – single page: A safety checklist is to be completed at time of 
laser activity (included) 
o Quality Control Safety Checklist – multiple pages: A safety checklist is to be completed at time of 
laser activity 
o Quality Control Safety Checklist Spreadsheet: To be completed each time there is laser activity 
o Health and Safety Report includes Risk Assessment: A risk assessment describing all safety 
hazards and recommended mitigation of identified risks includes show information, description 
of lasers and effects to be used, diagrams of location of lasers, proof of training, and insurance

certificates to be (NOTE: Not included in Appendix due to length. Contact LSS if you want a 
digital copy). 
o Sample Operation Log Sheet: Record of SOP followed 
o Sample Outdoor Operation Log Sheet: Record of SOPs followed for outdoor shows

Note: The following states and local agencies have unique registration/notification/licensing requirements. We 
recommend you contact them prior to operating in them for most recent regulatory requirements. 
States: 
Arizona: https://azdhs.gov/licensing/radiation-regulatory/index.php/xray#nir-light-show  
Florida: http://www.floridahealth.gov/search/search.cgi?zoomquery=laser+shows&zoomcat%5B%5D=0  
Illinois: https://www2.illinois.gov/iema/NRS/RadSafety/Pages/Laser.aspx  
New York: https://labor.ny.gov/workerprotection/safetyhealth/sh50.shtm  
Massachusetts: https://www.mass.gov/how-to/register-a-laser-device  
Texas: https://dshs.texas.gov/radiation/lasers/registration.aspx  
City Fire and Rescue Forms:  
New York City – Permit required for Theatrical Fog/Haze plus must have a person with Certificate of Fitness (COF): 
https://www1.nyc.gov/site/fdny/business/all-certifications/certificates-of-fitness.page  
Orlando, FL and/or Orange County Fire  
Cleveland, OH 
Las Vegas, NV: Clark County Fire 
Portland, OR

Pursuant to Chapter 501.122, Florida Statutes, and Chapter 64E-4, Florida Administrative Code, all
laser devices, except those exempted below, must be registered by the State of Florida.  This
applies to both users and manufacturers.
DEFINITIONS:
Medium - A substance that can provide gain for laser oscillation.  Common examples are Argon,
Krypton, Argon-Krypton, Nd-YAG, Helium-Neon, and Dye.
Pulse Duration - The time increment measured between the half-peak power points at the leading
and trailing edges of a pulse.
Continuous Wave (CW) - A laser operating with a continuous output for a time period greater than
or equal to 0.25 seconds. Lasers with outputs shorter than 0.25 seconds are classified as pulsed
lasers. Some lasers with variable length outputs can fit into both the CW and pulsed categories.
Wave Length - The distance measured between two points on a (periodic) wave which have the
same phase, usually measured in nanometers (nm). The visible light spectrum (the colors blue,
green, yellow, orange, and red) corresponds to waves having wavelengths between 400nm and
700nm.
Power - The time rate at which energy is given off, usually measured in watts (W). Pulsed lasers
are not often labeled with their output power.  Instead they are labeled with the output energy of
each pulse, usually measured in joules (J) or millijoules (mJ).
CLASS IV LASER DEVICES:  Class IV laser devices or products are those with an output power of
greater than or equal to 0.5 watts.  Examples of some class IV laser devices are all types of medical
laser surgery devices, advanced laser light show devices, laser cutters and drilling devices and
laser welding devices.
CLASS IIIB LASER DEVICES:  Class IIIB laser devices or products are those with an output of
greater than 0.005 watts, but less than 0.5 watts.  Examples of some class IIIB devices are some
small laser light shows, surveying devices, range finders, construction units, and road grading
equipment.
DETERMINING CLASS OF LASER DEVICE:
If your laser device class is not known you may find the class label on the housing of the laser
device, or inquire from the manufacturer.
EXEMPT FROM REGISTRATION:
All Class I, II, IIA, and IIIA laser products, except for those that allow access to other classes of
radiation during servicing, are exempted from registration and regulation, provided that the laser
product is maintained as a Class I, II, IIA, or IIIA laser product throughout its useful life.  These
regulations do not apply to lasers in storage, during shipment or sale, provided such lasers are
inoperable or not operated.

COMPLETING REGISTRATION FORMS:
The enclosed form must be filled out completely.  A laser safety officer must be designated, and
listed on the registration form.  A laser safety officer (LSO) means any individual, qualified by
training and experience in the evaluation and control of laser hazards, who is designated by the
registrant and has the authority and responsibility to establish and administer the laser radiation
protection program for a particular facility or a particular mobile laser.
REGISTRATION OF OUT-OF-STATE LASER RADIATION SOURCES:
Pursuant to Chapter 64E-4.005, Florida Administrative Code requires any person proposing to
bring any laser device (in classes other than those exempt from registration) into the State, for
temporary or permanent use (i.e. entertainment lasers for laser light shows, etc.) must give written
notice to the Department of Health, Bureau of Radiation Control a minimum of 20 days prior to such
device being used in the State.
REGISTRATION OF DEVICES AT SEPARATE LOCATION:
A separate registration is required for each location with a laser device.  Subsequent registrations
for each facility or mobile laser facility are required whenever additional laser devices are installed,
or whenever previously registered laser devices are disposed of, altered, destroyed or moved to
new locations.
DEADLINES FOR REGISTRATION OF LASER DEVICES AND PRODUCTS:
Registration of laser devices and products must be submitted to the department within 30 days after
acquisition of the laser device or products.
REGISTRATION FEE:
None
FREQUENCY OF REGISTRATION:
This is intended to be a one time only registration; however, if a laser device or product is altered,
moved to another location, destroyed or sold, the Department must be notified and the device
needs to be registered by the new owner or removed from the registration when destroyed.
RETURN FORM TO:
Department of Health
Bureau of Radiation Control
Laser Registration
Bin C21
4052 Bald Cypress Way
Tallahassee, Florida   32399-1741
Please mail both the white and yellow copies of the form to the Department.  When laser
registration forms have been processed, the yellow copy will be returned to you for proof of
registration.
